key_factors: Norway is ranked 23rd in the FIFA World Rankings, while Ivory Coast is ranked 31st.
This is the first-ever meeting between Ivory Coast and Norway in both competitive and friendly fixtures.
Norway starting right-back Julian Ryerson is officially ruled out of this match due to a thigh injury.
Norway rested key players Martin Ødegaard and Erling Haaland in their final group stage match against France, entering this match with a rest advantage.
No travel or weather asymmetry is present; the match is held under closed-roof, air-conditioned conditions at Dallas Stadium.

key_factors: France is ranked 3rd in the FIFA World Rankings, while Sweden is ranked 36th.
Sweden starting center back Isak Hien is officially ruled out of the tournament with a hamstring injury requiring surgery.

France has a significant travel advantage, traveling ~220 miles from Boston to New Jersey, while Sweden traveled ~1,380 miles from Dallas. This 1,160-mile difference exceeds the 800-mile threshold and warrants a 2-point adjustment.
A 3-point adjustment was applied for the injury asymmetry due to the absence of Sweden's key starter Isak Hien.
France forward Marcus Thuram is ruled out with a calf injury, but defender William Saliba is fit to return after recovering from a back injury.
Sweden has 4 full rest days compared to France's 3, which is under the 2-day threshold for adjustment.
Historical head-to-head record favors France with 12 wins, 5 draws, and 6 losses.

key_factors: Mexico is ranked 14th in the FIFA World Rankings (live 9th), while Ecuador is ranked 23rd.

Mexico has a significant travel advantage, remaining in Mexico City after their last match, while Ecuador traveled ~2,080 miles from New Jersey. This 2,080-mile travel difference exceeds the 800-mile threshold and warrants a 2-point adjustment.
Ecuador starting center back Piero Hincapié is doubtful with a muscle strain and undergoes a late fitness test.
Mexico has 5 full rest days compared to Ecuador's 4, which is under the 2-day threshold for adjustment.
Estadio Azteca is located at an altitude of 7,200 ft, which introduces fatigue risks, though both squads have significant historical exposure to altitude.
Heavy rainfall is forecast for Mexico City at kickoff, which may affect play style but affects both teams equally.
